docs.text: .image1{ border-radius: 100%; overflow: hidden; width: 135px; } .image2{ width:210px; overflow: hidden; border-radius:20%; } .image3{ width:95px; overflow: hidden; border-radius:5%; } .image4{ width:140px; overflow: hidden; border-radius:5%; } Guanxuan (Grayson) Jiang Feel free to reach out to me through any method; please refer to my contact information . I am seeking PhD opportunities worldwide for the Fall 2025 intake, particularly in the areas of HCI, HAI, CSCW, and Human Behavior. Please feel free to contact me if you have any openings! My name is Guanxuan Jiang (蒋冠宣), and I am an MPhil (Master of Philosophy) student in the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou) with a full scholarship. My research interests are in the fields of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Human Behavior, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR). I am member of Center for M etaverse and C omputational C reativity (MC 2 ) Lab . I obtained my Bachelor of Science with Honour from the University of Sheffield in the department of Computer Science with one-year Study Abroad at the Chinese University of Hong Kong , supported by the Global Opportunity Organization and the Turning Scheme. 蒋冠宣，香港科技大学（广州）哲学硕士（MPhil）学生，全额奖学金获得者。研究兴趣包括人机交互、人类行为分析、人工智能、虚拟现实和增强现实等领域。元宇宙与计算创意中心实验室（MC² Lab）的一员。于英国谢菲尔德大学计算机科学系获得荣誉理学学士学位，并在全球机会组织及转换计划的资助下，在香港中文大学进行了为期一年的海外学习。 Contact Information: Office: Room 318, Building E1, HKUST-GZ, Guangzhou, China Email: gjiang240[at]connect[dot]hkust-gz[dot]edu[dot]cn (academic) or jgxuann[at]outlook[dot]com Or anyway you can find me 😎 Recent News (Scroll) Aug 2024 : "Blending Social Interaction Realms: Harmonizing Online and Offline Interactions through Augmented Reality" @ ACM VINCI 2024. See you on 11th December in Hsinchu, Taiwan, China. Sept 2024 : "ChemistryVR: Enhancing Educational Experiences through Virtual Chemistry Lab Simulations" @ ACM SIGGRAPH ASIA 2024 Educator's Forum. See you on 3rd December in Tokyo, Japan. March 2024 : I passed my MPhil's PQA defense! Sept 2023 : I joined HKUSTGZ as a MPhil student! July 2023 : I graduated from the University of Sheffield with an Honored BSc.! May 2023 : I passed my thesis defense at University of Sheffield! Apr 2022 : I joined Xi'an Jiaotong Liverpool University as an research assistant. Aug 2021 : I started my summer school program at Peking University. Jan 2021 : I will spend one academic year (Yr2) at Chinese University of Hong Kong as the Study Abroad Program. Oct 2020 : I started my university life at the University of Sheffield. docs.text: Blog📓 Welcome to my blog space. I'm excited to share my life experiences and thoughts with all of you here. On this platform, I'll be posting updates regularly, offering interesting stories, insights, and my perspectives on various topics. I hope these posts resonate with you or provide some inspiration. I look forward to your subscriptions and interactions. Have a wonderful day, and thank you for your support! Big Day 🎉 I will document some of the big moments in my life here. I won't forget these times and the unforgettable memories they bring. University of Sheffield 🎓 Taken at the University of Sheffield, Sheffield, England on 17 July 2023. This is my graduation ceremony at the University of Sheffield. I'm thrilled to have completed my undergraduate studies here; it's a place filled with memories. I want to express my gratitude for everything that happened here—the friends I've made, the restaurants I've visited, and everything else. Anyway, thank you! Please refer to the video I uploaded on YouTube . Sketch🤔 🚧Coming soon... Trip ✈️ 🚧Coming soon... Technical Log 💻 🚧Coming soon... Page last revised on: 2024-08-21

docs.title: Technical Log 💻

docs.text: Selected Projects My research interests are board, encompassing, but not limited to, human-computer interaction, human-AI interaction, virtual reality, and augmented reality. Here, I will showcase some of my past work demos. If you find any of it insightful, please feel free to discuss and contact me. Virtual Reality ChemistryVR: Virtual Reality Chemistry Experiment Simulator These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2022. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Experiments play a crucial role in chemistry education, yet they often require extensive equipment, ample space, and pose potential safety hazards. Virtual Reality (VR) offers an innovative solution to these challenges through immersive and interactive simulations. In response, we developed a virtual laboratory aligned with the ninth-grade chemistry curriculum from the People's Education Press in China. This system encompasses safety training and detailed tutorials, allowing students to engage with interactive simulations and observe realistic experimental phenomena in a secure environment. By practicing chemistry experiments in VR, students can avoid dangerous and unforeseen outcomes resulting from experimental mistakes. Our system provides a risk-free method that effectively facilitates experiential and practice-oriented learning in chemistry. Furthermore, it seamlessly integrates theoretical knowledge with practical applications, closely following the official textbook. This comprehensive approach enables students to transition smoothly from theory to practice, deepening their understanding of chemistry concepts. As a result, students are better equipped for both standardized tests and real-world laboratory situations. Please refer to the demo video I uploaded on YouTube . Augmented Reality Blending Social Interaction Realms: Harmonizing Online and Offline Interactions through Augmented Reality These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2023. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Online social media has redefined human interaction, yet often at the expense of local engagement. To bridge this gap, we explored the innovative use of Augmented Reality (AR) to enhance location-specific interactions. We developed ARMessageBoard, a pioneering prototype that integrates AR with the user's surroundings to create virtual message boards. This approach aims to enrich the local context often overlooked by digital platforms. In our study with 15 participants, we demonstrated that combining AR with social media could better integrate virtual and real-world interactions, offering a new dimension to socio-digital exchanges. Please refer to the demo video I uploaded on YouTube . Human-AI Collabration 🚧Coming soon... Human Behaviour 🚧Coming soon... Human Computer Interaction 🚧Coming soon... Page last revised on: 2024-08-21
docs.title: Selected Projects

docs.text: These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2022. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Experiments play a crucial role in chemistry education, yet they often require extensive equipment, ample space, and pose potential safety hazards. Virtual Reality (VR) offers an innovative solution to these challenges through immersive and interactive simulations. In response, we developed a virtual laboratory aligned with the ninth-grade chemistry curriculum from the People's Education Press in China. This system encompasses safety training and detailed tutorials, allowing students to engage with interactive simulations and observe realistic experimental phenomena in a secure environment. By practicing chemistry experiments in VR, students can avoid dangerous and unforeseen outcomes resulting from experimental mistakes. Our system provides a risk-free method that effectively facilitates experiential and practice-oriented learning in chemistry. Furthermore, it seamlessly integrates theoretical knowledge with practical applications, closely following the official textbook. This comprehensive approach enables students to transition smoothly from theory to practice, deepening their understanding of chemistry concepts. As a result, students are better equipped for both standardized tests and real-world laboratory situations. Please refer to the demo video I uploaded on YouTube .

docs.text: These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2023. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Online social media has redefined human interaction, yet often at the expense of local engagement. To bridge this gap, we explored the innovative use of Augmented Reality (AR) to enhance location-specific interactions. We developed ARMessageBoard, a pioneering prototype that integrates AR with the user's surroundings to create virtual message boards. This approach aims to enrich the local context often overlooked by digital platforms. In our study with 15 participants, we demonstrated that combining AR with social media could better integrate virtual and real-world interactions, offering a new dimension to socio-digital exchanges.
